mcqs.question: What does Newton's First Law of Motion state?
Which of the following is an example of inertia?
If no net force acts on a moving object, it will:
Which of the following best exemplifies Newton's First Law?

fill_in_the_blanks.question: An object at rest tends to stay at rest, and an object in motion tends to stay in motion unless acted upon by a ________.
The property of an object to resist changes in its state of motion is called ________.
Newton's First Law is also known as the law of ________.
If a net external force is applied to an object, it will cause the object to ________.
A stationary object will remain ________ unless a force acts on it.

short_questions.question: What is Newton's First Law of Motion?
short_questions.answer: It states that an object will remain at rest or in uniform motion unless acted upon by an external force.

short_questions.question: How does inertia relate to mass?
short_questions.answer: Inertia is directly related to mass; greater mass means greater inertia.

short_questions.question: Can you give an everyday example of Newton's First Law?
short_questions.answer: A book on a table remains at rest until someone picks it up.

short_questions.question: What happens to a moving car if no brakes are applied?
short_questions.answer: It will continue to move at a constant speed in a straight line.

short_questions.question: Why do passengers lurch forward when a bus suddenly stops?
short_questions.answer: Due to inertia, their bodies tend to maintain their state of motion.
